The customer opens the food delivery app. He then sees the restaurants in his
                                        vicinity.
                                        He chooses his desired dishes and places the order. He applies any discount
                                        coupon if possible.
                                        He pays for the order via online (secure) or COD means. He gets to know the live
                                        order status via push notifications and delivery executive tracking features.
                                        The food is delivered to his home or the chosen location by the assigned
                                        delivery executive.

Food aggregator firms make money by:
                                        Charging delivery fees from their customers. Charging commissions on the profits
                                        earned by their partner restaurants.
                                        Getting commissions on desirable actions taken by customers on in-app ads.
                                        Collecting additional delivery charges during peak hours (scare availability of
                                        delivery executives), rain surge events, lockdowns, etcetera.

The types of food delivery apps are:
                                        Restaurant app/ cloud kitchen app: Here the restaurant or cloud kitchen has its
                                        own app for food delivery and takes care of the whole process of food delivery
                                        Food aggregator app: Here the business owner develops a food ordering app for
                                        his food delivery business. He partners with many restaurants and lists them on
                                        his app and he earns commissions on the profits made by the restaurants via his
                                        app
